rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope only when binding implementation-level details; use component type scope for abstract port-level contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by confining AGREE annexes to valid scopes defined by the original architecture.
Only reference features or ports explicitly exposed in the AADL model; avoid subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax and be placed within correct AADL component or implementation blocks.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with the actual data type of referenced ports—use real or int for numeric ranges, not Boolean.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables—they are not in scope unless mapped via explicit ports or constants.
Avoid translating Boolean or enumeration ports into numeric range checks unless the AADL model explicitly declares them as numeric scalars.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
